task: Write a Python function truncate_words(s, n) returning the first n whitespace-separated words of the string joined by single spaces, so runs of whitespace collapse. When n is zero or negative return ''. When the string has fewer than n words return them all. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided function meets all the criteria as expected.
trace:
I approached this task by first splitting the input string `s` into a list of words using the `split()` method. Then, I checked if the number of words in the list is less than `n`. If so, I joined all the words back together with spaces and returned the result. If not, I sliced the list to only include the first `n` words, then joined them and returned the result.

I handled one edge case where the number of words in the input string is less than `n`. In such cases, instead of throwing an exception or filling up with dummy data, the function simply returns all the available words separated by spaces.
